title: Manual Payroll Calculation
description: Calculate each line of the payslip from scratch, exactly as you would in the exam. All figures are rounded to 2 decimal places.

scenarios.title: Week 1 — Sarah O'Connor
scenarios.context: This is Sarah's first week. She has no previous cumulative figures.

steps.label: Gross Pay (this week)
steps.explanation: The employee's basic pay for the week — given in the scenario.

steps.label: Cumulative Gross Pay to Date (Week 1)
steps.explanation: Previous cumulative gross (€0) + this week's pay (€700) = €700.00

steps.label: Cumulative SRCOP to Date (Week 1 × €807.69)
steps.explanation: The standard rate cut-off for 1 week. All of Sarah's income this week is below €807.69, so all is taxed at 20%.

steps.label: Cumulative Tax @ 20% (on €700.00)
steps.explanation: €700.00 × 20% = €140.00. All income is below the SRCOP so the higher rate (40%) does not apply.

steps.label: Less: Cumulative Tax Credits (Week 1 × €72.12)
steps.explanation: 1 week × €72.12 = €72.12 total credits to date.

steps.label: Cumulative PAYE Due to Date
steps.explanation: Cumulative Tax (€140.00) − Cumulative Credits (€72.12) = €67.88

steps.label: Less: PAYE Already Paid (Previous Weeks)
steps.explanation: Week 1 — no previous weeks, so €0 already paid.

steps.label: PAYE Due This Week
steps.explanation: Cumulative PAYE Due (€67.88) − Previously Paid (€0.00) = €67.88

steps.label: PRSI @ 4% (gross €700.00 is above €352 threshold)
steps.explanation: €700.00 × 4% = €28.00. Sarah earns above €352/week so full Class A PRSI applies.

steps.label: USC: 0.5% on €231.00
steps.explanation: First USC band: €231.00 × 0.5% = €1.155, rounded to €1.16

steps.label: Net Pay (Gross − Total Deductions)
steps.explanation: €700.00 − €111.54 = €588.46. This is the amount Sarah takes home.

scenarios.title: Week 3 — Sarah O'Connor (Cumulative System)
scenarios.context: Same employee, same pay — but now we must use the CUMULATIVE system. Her figures for Weeks 1 and 2 are known. You need to calculate Week 3 PAYE based on total earnings to date, then subtract what was already paid.

steps.label: Gross Pay (Week 3)
steps.explanation: Same weekly pay as before.

steps.label: Cumulative Gross Pay to Date (End of Week 3)
steps.explanation: Previous cumulative gross €1,400 (Weeks 1+2) + this week €700 = €2,100.00

steps.label: Cumulative SRCOP to Date (3 × €807.69)
steps.explanation: 3 weeks × €807.69 = €2,423.07. Cumulative gross €2,100 is still below this — all income at 20%.

steps.label: Cumulative Tax @ 20% (on €2,100.00)
steps.explanation: €2,100.00 × 20% = €420.00. None of her cumulative income has exceeded the SRCOP yet.

steps.label: Less: Cumulative Tax Credits (3 × €72.12)
steps.explanation: 3 weeks × €72.12 = €216.36 total credits applied cumulatively.

steps.label: Cumulative PAYE Due to Date
steps.explanation: Cumulative Tax (€420.00) − Cumulative Credits (€216.36) = €203.64

steps.label: Less: PAYE Already Paid (Weeks 1 + 2)
steps.explanation: €67.88 (Week 1) + €67.88 (Week 2) = €135.76 already deducted and paid to Revenue.

steps.label: PAYE Due This Week (Week 3)
steps.explanation: Cumulative PAYE Due (€203.64) − Previously Paid (€135.76) = €67.88. Notice it's the same as Week 1 — consistent income means consistent weekly tax.

steps.label: PRSI @ 4% (on gross €700.00)
steps.explanation: PRSI is calculated on THIS week's gross only — it is NOT cumulative. €700.00 × 4% = €28.00

steps.label: USC: 0.5% on €231.00
steps.explanation: USC is also calculated weekly, not cumulatively. €231.00 × 0.5% = €1.16

steps.label: Total USC
steps.explanation: €1.16 + €5.29 + €9.21 = €15.66 — same as Week 1 since gross pay is the same.

steps.label: Net Pay (Gross − Total Deductions)
steps.explanation: €700.00 − €111.54 = €588.46. The cumulative system has kept her tax consistent each week.
